text: Wayspot stays small: the launcher owns one bounded picker lifecycle and the notification daemon owns the only long-lived interface.
Comments are planned production code; planning and memory are production artifacts.
Everything has a bound and each owner cleans up once.
Wayspot is a CLI-summoned SDL app launcher plus a minimal freedesktop notification daemon. The launcher starts, loads candidates, launches one selected command, cleans up once, and exits.
Reject GTK, shell modules, open provider registries, broad WM abstractions, web/window/workspace providers, runtime scripting VMs, and resident launcher IPC.
Use the local `.agent/history` surface as Wayspot memory and keep it exact, bounded, and free of expired planning chatter.

The coordinating agent owns approval of Wayspot work proposals before execution. Approved proposals must pin objective, files, owners, non-goals, bounds, cleanup, tests, comments, memory impact, and stop conditions.
